conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules to make sure I can give you accurate guidance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To assess this properly, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for, and if so, how many?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex and he's 13 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have one child, Alex, who is 13 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few more questions:

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK at the moment. I'm currently living in France.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living in France, this affects your eligibility for Child Benefit.

According to the rules, you may be able to get Child Benefit if you live in certain countries. Could you tell me:

Are you a Crown servant (for example, working for the UK government or diplomatic services abroad)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. I'm just living here privately.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. 

Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have any status under the EU Settlement Scheme. I moved to France after Brexit and I'm just living here as a regular resident.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me ask one more question to complete my assessment:

Does Alex live with you in France, or does he live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with me here in France.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the information you've given me:
- You have one child, Alex, who is 13 years old
- You live in France (not the UK)
- You are not a Crown servant
- You do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- Alex lives with you in France

Let me now provide my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete**

I'm sorry to inform you that you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

Here's why: While Alex meets the age requirement at 13 years old, the main issue is the residency requirement. To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally need to live in the UK. 

There are some exceptions that allow people living abroad to claim Child Benefit, such as:
- Being a Crown servant (working for the UK government or diplomatic services)
- Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme

Unfortunately, since you moved to France after Brexit as a regular resident and don't have any of these special statuses, you don't meet the residency requirement for claiming Child Benefit.

If your circumstances change in the future - for example, if you move back to the UK - you would then be able to claim Child Benefit for Alex (as long as he remains under 16, or under 20 if in approved education or training).

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old, which meets the age requirement (under 16). However, the claimant lives in France and is not a Crown servant. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 AND you live in the UK. While there are exceptions for people living abroad (such as Crown servants or those with settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme), the claimant does not meet any of these exceptions. The claimant moved to France after Brexit as a regular resident without EU Settlement Scheme status. Therefore, the claimant does not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit because they do not meet the residency requirement. While Alex meets the age criteria (13 years old) and lives with the claimant, the claimant lives in France and does not qualify for any of the exceptions that would allow someone living abroad to claim Child Benefit (not a Crown servant, no settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme).

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 13 years old, which meets the age requirement (under 16). However, the claimant lives in France and is not a Crown servant. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 AND you live in the UK. While there are exceptions for people living abroad (such as Crown servants or those with settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme), the claimant does not meet any of these exceptions. The claimant moved to France after Brexit as a regular resident without EU Settlement Scheme status. Therefore, the claimant does not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit because they do not meet the residency requirement. While Alex meets the age criteria (13 years old) and lives with the claimant, the claimant lives in France and does not qualify for any of the exceptions that would allow someone living abroad to claim Child Benefit (not a Crown servant, no settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme).
